role_title: IAM Strategy and Operations Business Analyst , Assistant Vice President

level_signals: Compensation range $80,000–$140,000 annual, consistent with mid-level IC at a large financial institution
Role supports Global Head of IAM but does not indicate direct reports

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in a business analyst, strategy, or business operations role within a complex, multi‑stakeholder environment
Proven ability to organize and structure business inputs into clear documentation such as process maps, operating model views, and portfolio or initiative summaries
Experience supporting portfolio-style work, including tracking multiple concurrent initiatives, coordinating updates, and preparing materials for governance forums
Strong analytical skills with the ability to work with data, identify trends, and produce clear, executive‑ready insights and summaries
Experience supporting financial activities such as budget or forecast inputs, variance tracking, or spend oversight
Familiarity with reporting and dashboard concepts and comfort working with metrics related to delivery, risk, or financial performance
Excellent written and oral communication skills, including the ability to translate complex information into concise, understandable content for diverse stakeholders
Strong stakeholder management and facilitation skills, with the ability to build collaborative relationships across functions and levels
Demonstrated planning, organization, and prioritization skills, with the ability to manage deadlines in a fast-moving environment
Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with distributed and cross‑functional teams
Competent using standard productivity and collaboration tools to support documentation, analysis, and reporting

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Advanced Excel skills (e.g., VLOOKUP, pivot tables, and beyond), impactful PowerPoint competency, and experience with Power BI are a plus
Familiarity with identity and access management, cybersecurity, or technology risk and governance concepts is desirable

scope.team_size_estimate: small team supporting Global Head of IAM
scope.surface: IAM Strategy & Operations portfolio governance, reporting, process improvement, and financial oversight
primary_metrics_inferred: Portfolio delivery health and status tracking
Governance forum readiness and reporting quality
core_responsibilities: Support portfolio governance by maintaining views of the IAM book of work, tracking status, dependencies, risks, and issues
Coordinate business case and initiative documentation
Facilitate and document process mapping activities
Support strategy and planning activities for senior leadership reviews
Provide financial oversight support across the portfolio
Develop and enhance metrics and dashboards
Create and maintain guides, playbooks, and standard templates
Maintain organized knowledge repositories and trackers
Enable cross-training and onboarding within the team

green_flags: Clear role scope supporting Global Head of IAM in a defined small team
Comprehensive benefits program described including 401K match, medical, dental, vision, PTO
Role is pivotal to a growing IAM portfolio — visibility and growth potential implied
IAM domain knowledge listed as desirable rather than required, lowering barrier to entry
red_flags: No years of experience minimum stated — seniority expectations unclear despite AVP title
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Wide compensation range ($80K–$140K) may indicate significant variance in how the role is leveled internally
SOP98 reference is highly specific regulatory terminology not explained in JD — candidates unfamiliar with it may be disadvantaged
Location listed as Quincy, MA (onsite implied by URL) but remote/hybrid policy not explicitly stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from AVP title in role name and comp range; years not stated)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no mention of direct reports)

ambiguities: Work location type is not stated in the JD body. The URL includes 'Quincy-Massachusetts' suggesting onsite, but hybrid or remote eligibility is not explicitly addressed. Treated as onsite here.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Marked as 'unclear'.
SOP98 is referenced as a key responsibility area but is not defined in the JD — it likely refers to State Street's internal control standard; candidates may need to research this.
